sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel data documented in sources

special_mechanics.name: Tune-up Items
special_mechanics.description: Collectible performance upgrades: turbo acceleration, higher top speed ('hotter engines'), and improved traction/cornering ('super sticky tires')

special_mechanics.name: Vehicle Upgrades
special_mechanics.description: Three vehicle tiers with increasing speed: standard truck → 4-Wheeler → Off Roader

special_mechanics.name: Hazard Physics
special_mechanics.description: Environmental effects: oil slicks cause loss of control spin-out, water puddles and rain slow vehicles, pop-up barriers cause crashes

special_mechanics.name: Weapon System
special_mechanics.description: Missiles disable opponents from front, bombs from rear; temporarily disable vehicles

special_mechanics.name: Speed Boost Zippers
special_mechanics.description: Stationary collectible items that provide extra speed boost when driven over

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. Sources document gameplay mechanics and vehicle balancing (e.g., 127 mph max opponent speed, vehicle tier progression) but do not provide technical physics constants (gravity, acceleration values, sub-pixel system). Game praised for smooth controls and responsive handling in period reviews.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - gameplay mechanics and vehicle system
wikipedia - gameplay details, vehicle upgrades, hazard mechanics, weapon system
